merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/click/core.py b/src/click/core.py
index 666ad68..c585d0c 100644
--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import collections.abc as cabc
+import copy
 import enum
 import errno
 import inspect
@@ -228,6 +229,11 @@ class Context:
         value is not set, it defaults to the value from the parent
         context. ``Command.show_default`` overrides this default for the
         specific command.
+    :param validate_nesting: If enabled, the context will track child
+        contexts created through :meth:`_make_sub_context` and raise a
+        ``RuntimeError`` if it is closed while any of those children
+        are still open. Child contexts automatically inherit this
+        setting from their parent.
 
     .. versionchanged:: 8.2
         The ``protected_args`` attribute is deprecated and will be removed in
@@ -280,6 +286,7 @@ class Context:
         token_normalize_func: t.Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
         color: bool | None = None,
         show_default: bool | None = None,
+        validate_nesting: bool = False,
     ) -> None:
         #: the parent context or `None` if none exists.
         self.parent = parent
@@ -432,6 +439,15 @@ class Context:
         self._parameter_source: dict[str, ParameterSource] = {}
         self._exit_stack = ExitStack()
 
+        #: If enabled, this context will track child contexts created
+        #: via :meth:`_make_sub_context` and raise a ``RuntimeError``
+        #: when closed while any of them are still open.
+        self.validate_nesting = validate_nesting
+        #: Child contexts created via :meth:`_make_sub_context` that
+        #: are still open. Only populated when :attr:`validate_nesting`
+        #: is enabled.
+        self._open_child_contexts: list[Context] = []
+
     @property
     def protected_args(self) -> list[str]:
         import warnings
@@ -465,6 +481,51 @@ class Context:
             "auto_envvar_prefix": self.auto_envvar_prefix,
         }
 
+    def save_state(self) -> dict[str, t.Any]:
+        """Capture a snapshot of the current context's mutable state.
+
+        The returned dictionary can later be passed to
+        :meth:`restore_state` to revert the context back to this
+        state. Attributes that could hold nested, mutable data
+        (:attr:`params`, :attr:`obj`, :attr:`meta`, :attr:`default_map`,
+        and the internal parameter source mapping) are deep copied so
+        that later modifications to those objects do not affect the
+        saved snapshot.
+
+        .. versionadded:: 8.3
+        """
+        return {
+            "params": copy.deepcopy(self.params),
+            "args": self.args.copy(),
+            "protected_args": self._protected_args.copy(),
+            "opt_prefixes": set(self._opt_prefixes),
+            "obj": copy.deepcopy(self.obj),
+            "meta": copy.deepcopy(self._meta),
+            "default_map": copy.deepcopy(self.default_map),
+            "parameter_source": copy.deepcopy(self._parameter_source),
+            "invoked_subcommand": self.invoked_subcommand,
+            "resilient_parsing": self.resilient_parsing,
+        }
+
+    def restore_state(self, snapshot: dict[str, t.Any]) -> None:
+        """Restore context state previously captured with
+        :meth:`save_state`.
+
+        :param snapshot: The dictionary returned by :meth:`save_state`.
+
+        .. versionadded:: 8.3
+        """
+        self.params = snapshot["params"]
+        self.args = snapshot["args"]
+        self._protected_args = snapshot["protected_args"]
+        self._opt_prefixes = snapshot["opt_prefixes"]
+        self.obj = snapshot["obj"]
+        self._meta = snapshot["meta"]
+        self.default_map = snapshot["default_map"]
+        self._parameter_source = snapshot["parameter_source"]
+        self.invoked_subcommand = snapshot["invoked_subcommand"]
+        self.resilient_parsing = snapshot["resilient_parsing"]
+
     def __enter__(self) -> Context:
         self._depth += 1
         push_context(self)
@@ -606,11 +667,34 @@ class Context:
         """Invoke all close callbacks registered with
         :meth:`call_on_close`, and exit all context managers entered
         with :meth:`with_resource`.
+
+        :raises RuntimeError: if :attr:`validate_nesting` is enabled
+            and one or more child contexts created through
+            :meth:`_make_sub_context` are still open.
+
+        .. versionchanged:: 8.2
+            Validates that open child contexts are closed first when
+            :attr:`validate_nesting` is enabled.
         """
+        if self.validate_nesting and self._open_child_contexts:
+            open_names = ", ".join(
+                repr(child.info_name) for child in self._open_child_contexts
+            )
+            raise RuntimeError(
+                "Tried to close context, but it still has open child"
+                f" contexts: {open_names}."
+

    def test_context_state_snapshot_all_attributes():
        """Test that all relevant context attributes are captured and restored."""
        import click
    
        @click.command()
        @click.option("--verbose", is_flag=True)
        def cli(verbose):
            pass
    
        ctx = cli.make_context("cli", ["--verbose"])
    
        # Modify various attributes
        ctx.terminal_width = 120
        ctx.max_content_width = 100
        ctx.allow_extra_args = True
        ctx.allow_interspersed_args = False
        ctx.ignore_unknown_options = True
        ctx.resilient_parsing = True
        ctx.auto_envvar_prefix = "TEST"
        ctx.color = True
        ctx.show_default = True
        ctx.invoked_subcommand = "test"
    
        snapshot = ctx.save_state()
    
        # Change attributes
        ctx.terminal_width = 80
        ctx.max_content_width = 60
        ctx.allow_extra_args = False
        ctx.allow_interspersed_args = True
        ctx.ignore_unknown_options = False
        ctx.resilient_parsing = False
        ctx.auto_envvar_prefix = "OTHER"
        ctx.color = False
        ctx.show_default = False
        ctx.invoked_subcommand = None
    
        # Restore
        ctx.restore_state(snapshot)
    
        # Verify restoration
>       assert ctx.terminal_width == 120
E       assert 80 == 120
E        +  where 80 = <click.core.Context object at 0x7040fddc3290>.terminal_width
